title: Portix's Better End Fight
description: Make the end fight more challenging!
body:
## Have you ever felt like the Ender Dragon fight is just too easy?
**Look no further** — this mod overhauls the dull parts of the vanilla End fight and turns it into a more strategic and challenging experience!

The dragon is now **deeply connected to its End Crystals**, becoming **invincible while any crystals are active**, and **able to respawn them** at key health thresholds — turning the battle into a true test of endurance and planning.

But the crystals aren't gonna be that easy to destroy, because **only one is vulnerable at a time**, making this even more strategic


### What this mod does: 
- **Makes the dragon invincible** while any End Crystals remain.

- **Automatically respawns crystals** when the dragon reaches **80%, 60%, 40%, or 20%** health — if a configurable cooldown has passed.

- **Grants explosion immunity** to the dragon to prevent cheesing.

- **Switches the crystal vulnerability around** after a configurable amount of time, so it's more interesting


### Features:
- Visual effects, including **particles around invincible towers and custom crystal respawn visuals.**

- Should be compatible with any **End-related mods or datapacks.**

- Configurable via MidnightLib **(required)**.

<details>
<summary>Command</summary>

- /endfightoverhaul crystalRespawnCooldown - non-op command that shows the remaining time until the towers can respawn again

</details>


### Known issues:

- When you enter The End, the crystals are all vulnerable, and take the cooldown time to become invincible. This is a bug I'm aware of, and I haven't yet figured out a good way to fix this issue.
  
- The particles around the towers can take a few seconds to disappear, so it might be a little harder to spot the right tower to target immediately. This is because the particles are longer-lasting, due to their need to last long enough to create the immersive shield effect around the towers.


This mod requires MidnightLib for the config.
